reviews.review_text: look at the hours i have in this game, and ask to yourself why i would spend this much time in a bad game, that is all

"Batman: Arkham Knight" is an amazing game, with an engaging story, smooth combat, and an incredible atmosphere. Gotham City is beautiful to explore, and the gameplay truly makes you feel like Batman.

As usual, another masterpiece in the Arkham series. They don't make games like these anymore. The batmobile challenges can be frustrating and difficult as can the Riddler puzzles. But I suppose that's the point. Great visuals and story.

the game is so counter-intuitive that it makes me want to slam my head into a wall

(sexy characters though so 10/10 would recommend)

[h1] THE BRUTAL AND GORGEOUS FINALE [/h1]

[b][i]  A visually stunning, dark masterclass in gameplay design that still looks better than most modern titles. The addition of the Batmobile adds a whole new layer of high-octane destruction, and the combat and stealth are at their absolute mechanical peak. A grand, emotional, and brutal conclusion to the legendary trilogy.

This is how the Batman died. 9.5/10  [/i][/b]

stunning graphics with mid story that consist too much batmobile gameplay and extremely hard enemy even at easiest settings but suprisingly many contents and side mission plus skins from various batman movies overall i stiill think arkham city is a much better game

Some games let you play a hero. Arkham Knight lets you understand the cost of being one. Think about that.
Peak Entertainment.
This is the best superhero video game hands down. Sure there are a few criticisms one can have. But the game overall is amazing.
Absolute peak, one of the best games I've ever played and replayed and replayed and replayed.
best batman game ever. i feel bad for the bad guys in this game, you can relentlessly beat them down to a pulp.

Some of the quests are very rough around the edges, and the story could've been handled much better; but man this game has the smoothest gameplay of every Arkham game by far. Not only that, but the graphics put a lot of modern games to shame.

Not perfect, but easily my favourite game.

I don't care what anyone says. This game is a masterpiece for what it is. I like the Batmobile and I don't know what people have against it. Though a combat-mode-toggle would have been useful. Every time when I try to brake on the racing courses (with the Arkham Knight Batmobile), I press L2 and then lose all of my momenum.
Anyways I enjoy this game maybe the most of all Arkham games. 10/10 (MUST PLAY!!)

An awesome finale to the Arkham trilogy. One of my favorite games of all time. 

My only issue with the game is that it relies heavily on the batmobile. Don't get me wrong, the batmobile controls really well and it's really fun to drive around the city and mow every obstacle in your way, but i think there is a little too much of it. Like for example, there is a side mission where you have to disable bombs scattered around Gotham with your batmobile. When you start to hack the bomb, it starts an encounter where you have to destroy the militia drones. First it is okay. There is only couple of them, but when you are disabling the last bombs there comes like 50 of them. And i think there is way too many bombs for the gameplay they offer. They could have cut some of the bombs out and made the Deacon Blackfire side mission longer. 

The combat feels super fluid in this game. They fleshed out the already great Arkham city combat and gave you even more gadgets to use. The fear multitakedown is really great addition to the predator segments and let's the encounters be larger in enemy size and location. 

The DLC stories and missions are really good. The season of infamy missions are all good and give closure to some of the best characters in the trilogy. I especially liked the Mr.Freeze and Raz al Ghul missions. The Mr.Freeze missions captures what makes him a compelling character and an interesting villain and gives him and his wife Nora the most beautiful ending they could have. Then there are the extra story DLC. All of them are quite short except for one, but i'll get to that in a bit. The short ones are all good for their length. The Catwoman and Harley Quinn stories are quite underwhelming, which i hate to say because Harley is my favorite DC character. The Nightwing and Robin DLC are good and i really like the voice acting and dialogue in the Robin DLC. The longer DLC story A Matter of family is amazing. The gameplay of Batgirl is well made and differentiated from Batmans gameplay and she feels fresh and not the same as Batman. The story is fun an has a new small open world to explore. 

In conclusion i think this is the pinnacle of the Arkham series. I like it better than Arkham city.

This is still one of the best free-flow combat games of all time, and it still shows. When this game is on sale or as a bundle during a major steam sale, make sure to get it! you wont regret it and you will have so much fun punching criminals and drifting around Gotham in the batmobile.

Got it on PlayStation: loved it.

Got it on steam: loved it more.

You feel like Batman and the games story truly makes the difference.

I hate the riddler but I love everything else.

The game I will never forget and the game which made the little kid in me smile.

I got the DLC and I think that made the experience so much better.

Do as you want but this game is amazing.
runs well on my jank ass laptop. newest lego batman, you will never be arkham knight
A great sendoff for an excellent trilogy. The presentation, art direction, and graphics would be considered best in class and cutting edge even today.
Great Game even after years I'm glad to see such games still holding up. I wish we get more of these games, not the Gotham Knights.

Amazing game honestly the best superhero game to ever be made only problem was the tank was a tad overused but other than that it runs great, the combat is varied and amazing, bat-mobile (despite it being overused) is still very fun and, the gadgets are amazing, so is the side content I mean I spent a solid 8+ hours just doing side stuff it is very fun and addicting 10/10 would recommend.

Great game, nice lead up from Arkham Asylum, City, and origins. Saving Knight for last was the best thing to do! Still... The BATGIRL DLC kept giving me a "FATAL ERROR" :( It still completed the achievement at least... Anyway, 10/10 game, was great to stream!
- R3dF0x_VT

Absolute Cinema. If you've played Insomniac Spider-Man, I'd say this is better. Incredible trilogy and beautiful game to finish it. Combat, story, and puzzles have been fully refined. Even just the Batmobile alone has like 40 different mechanics for puzzles.

Having recently played Asylum and City, Knight feels much more modern than those. It is filled with content and different things to do, and a larger world to explore, and the graphics are greatly improved. It's very atmospheric, really giving that comic book vibe.
I really loved gliding around Gotham, stumbling upon missions as I did.
The main story is very enjoyable, but some of the side missions are a bit tedious and repetitive.
I found the combat easier than the previous games, and slightly less enjoyable. Some stealth/strategy parts were fun, such as planning out how to use the disruptor effectively, and adapting to the different enemy types during combat. But most of the boss fights were more annoying than challenging.
My biggest gripe with this game however, is the Batmobile, since the game doesn't include it as an option or a strategic alternative, but many missions require you to use it. I can get behind using it for puzzle solving, which it does a few times, but I really didn't enjoy the numerous tank fights or the pursuit sequences.

The story was a great conclusion to all the arkham games even if I'm playing it ten years late. The gameplay for the most part felt like the best version of all the arkham games however the tank stuff did get old fast but it was never awful. Did crash a lot and got stuck a few times in spots and had to reload so still has a few glitches.

Batman: Arkham Knight – 4/5

This game was so good!

For me, this was easily my favorite entry in the *Arkham* series. Everything felt a little more refined than in the previous games, which made this one incredibly satisfying to play. The combat, atmosphere, and story were all fantastic and kept pulling me back in to clear every mission I could find. While some players didn't care for the Batmobile, and I agree its use was a little excessive, overall I still liked it and felt like it added a cool new dimension to the gameplay.
Gameplay:

This is one of the most fun combat games I have ever played. The fighting felt remarkably smooth and genuinely made you feel powerful. The stealth mechanics were also a massive step up from the previous titles; I never felt bogged down or stuck in predator encounters, which kept the momentum going.

The addition of fear takedowns was an absolute blast because instead of meticulously picking off every last guard, I could just drop down and instantly eliminate five enemies at once. I also really appreciated the disruptor gadget. It allowed me to disable weapons before dropping into a brawl, saving me from instantly getting gunned down which was a frustration I ran into a lot in the older games.

Just like the combat, the traversal was incredibly fun. Once I fully upgraded the grapple launch, flying around Gotham felt amazing. Because the core movement and fighting mechanics were so strong, I stayed completely engaged and had fun with the side missions, even when the objectives themselves started to feel a bit repetitive.

Story and Atmosphere:

The story was really compelling and kept me hooked, especially as everything built toward the finale. I absolutely loved the twists near the end. Gotham itself was a stunning place to explore, and the open world was brilliant. I had a great time navigating the city to track down various quests and side stories.

I also really appreciated the mission select wheel. It made tracking my progress seamless, ensuring I always had a clear objective and never felt lost or stuck on what to do next.

Overall:

Ultimately, Arkham Knight is a phenomenal experience and stands out as my favorite in the trilogy. Coming from a gaming background where I usually prefer a purely stealthy approach, this was a blast because it let me play like a shadow, but didn't punish me when it was time to transition into a massive fistfight. It is easily the most polished game in the series, and it remains incredibly fun from start to finish.

I've finally arrived to the finale of the Arkham series. A trip to the madhouse and a stint in a mega-prison has all led up to what I believe is the ultimate culmination of the talent Rocksteady once had on how to do a Batman game proper.

The gameplay continues offering the fantasy of donning the cowl and becoming the caped crusader. Not only is the freeflow combat still satisfying, but is further improved upon. You feel the weight of every kick and punch you land as you snap from one poor bastard to the next in a wonderful dance of sheer brutality. But Batman is more than just his fists, as the game shows off his stealth and detective capabilities in spades, while also having some new toys to play with.

Though there are still some quirks to it. Enemies still like to flash step to land a punch on you when you thought could avoid it, really having you need lightning fast reaction time to avoid losing your combo. And it took some time to wrangle the batmobile controls.

For a game that came out more than a decade ago, the visuals are stunning, probably the best any Arkham game has ever looked, which is saying something considering how the earlier games in the series still look leagues better than what most games coming out today look like (go figure). You could fly around the Gotham Islands for hours and never get tired of soaking in the scenery. It also does something bold by straying away from the slightly cartoonish stylings for a more realistic aesthetic that matches the grim story and themes.

The main story of the game is pretty compelling, though with some odd choices (i.e. the stark change to Scarecrow's character). But honestly, it was the cast and side missions that I loved the most. Seeing Bruce at his lowest after the fallout of Arkham City is really heightened by Kevin Conroy's performance. He puts in his all in conveying the kind of anger and sorrow from a man at the end of his rope, but never letting go. Along with familiar and new faces that keep Gotham feeling alive and kinetic, shifting the world space as you explore and uncover.

If you're a sado-masochist- I mean, completionist like myself, then you'll have a hell of a time with this game. Eddie's back on his ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ again with his Riddler trophies, which is tied to 100%'ing the game. Along with a nigh endless amount of challenges with their own requirements if you wanna go for all the achievements. Hope you enjoy race tracks.

Oh, and of course, the modding capabilities. While the previous games were - understandably - pretty barren when it comes to the modding scene, Knight has a plethora of mods that has something for everyone to enjoy. And I do mean everyone.

This truly was Rocksteady's swan song, and I'm glad that nothing that could tarnish the legacy of Batman came out afterwards. Nope, nothing at all!

Overall, I give Batman: Arkham Knight a 9/10.

This is one of the best games I have ever played, the combat in this game is incredible, and the scenery throughout the story is just amazing. Sometimes you can really get stuck on some missions but I feel like that is the point of the Arkham games. To think like batman, if you get stuck on a mission you have to think like a detective and figure it out. That is what I love so much about this game.
Great game and great storyline. Feels exciting and clean even 11 years after release! Works well with Crossover 26 for Mac players, occasional glitch but nothing that impacts gameplay

The game launch might have been rough but it still competes with a lot of the latest titles inn terms of gameplay, graphical fidelity. The story ending is a bit rushed. But the have nailed the vibe of being the dark knight. Hate the riddler trophies.

Batman: Arkham Knight is the most ambitious and content-rich entry in the series - and a fitting finale. It builds on everything that came before, delivering a huge amount of content, strong storytelling, and impressive technical polish. The Batmobile is the big addition here, and for the most part, it’s fantastic (imho despite many griping about it) - chases, combat, and traversal all feel great, though some of the Riddler racetrack challenges overstay their welcome. The game features a stacked roster of villains, with Scarecrow taking a well-deserved spotlight and the Joker still playing a major role in a clever way. The Arkham Knight is an interesting addition, even if his identity isn’t hard to figure out. One downside is the lack of traditional boss fights - most major encounters lean more toward set-piece battles than direct one-on-one showdowns. Still, those are minor complaints in an otherwise stellar package. It brings the story full circle and stands as one of the best superhero games ever made - a powerful conclusion to an incredible series.

in my opinion the best superhero game of all time. many games try to follow the same sauce this game created. still holds the test of time to this day. the batmobile missions do get pretty annoying. but thats my only gripe.
Apart from the irritating cloudburst tank, which was okayish and challenging but just kept repeating itself over and over again, I give this three BIG BOOMS.

Batman: Arkham Knight makes you feel like the ultimate Batman.

The graphics still look insanely good even today, Gotham City is beautiful and dark, and gliding through the city at night feels incredible. The combat and stealth are smoother than ever, making every fight feel satisfying.

The Batmobile gets mixed opinions, but personally it made the game feel even more cinematic and added scale to Gotham. And when the game focuses on Batman’s mind slowly breaking under pressure, the story becomes genuinely intense.

Kevin Conroy’s Batman and Mark Hamill’s Joker are absolutely legendary here.

Amazing atmosphere, fantastic gameplay, and one of the best superhero experiences ever made.

A perfect finale to the Arkham trilogy.

Batman Arkham Knight is a truly stunning game, and might I add probably one of the greatest games I have ever played. From the visuals, to the combat, to even the increasingly-repetitive tank missions, Batman: Arkham Knight does it all, and still tops even today's modern games. I find myself replaying this game a lot just because of how fun and amazing it is. Would truly recommend if you're a Batman fan, and a video game lover of sorts.
Good game, its very underrated, highly recommended to play, Good for low end PCs.

After doing 100% twice in this game and getting every achivement I can with clear concience say that there just isn't anything that comes close to scratching that itch these days. Being batman feels amazing, you're cool, badass and feel like the toughest bastard in any room. Gadgets make combat fresh every few hours and side content is honestly peak. If you aren't a batman hater you'll probably enjoy this game immensely.

The Batman: Arkham series is the benchmark for superhero games, and Arkham Knight is the perfect finale. Amazing combat, a fantastic story, stunning graphics that still hold up today, and Gotham City feels alive like no other superhero game. Even years later, many games still struggle to match what Rocksteady achieved with the Arkham series

very great game cool fun and kinda challenging  also cool puzzles

This is a fantastic game.

It has action, stealth, puzzles, collectibles, racing, car chases, tank battles, amazing set pieces, well written characters, and a great story. Takes 40 hours to complete the main story, side missions, and do the DLC. There's a huge variety of things to do, so you rarely feel like it's repetitive.

The biggest downside is the Riddler stuff. It's mostly good, but there's roughly 250 Riddler collectables and puzzles. It starts getting old after only 50. People say the batmobile is overused, and I tend to agree, but it's still fun and plays well. The DLC stories feel underbaked, but have some good moments. There are some audio glitches and visual flickering from time to time that lasts a few seconds, but the technical issues are infrequent.

This is one of the most satisfying games to play. Its few weaknesses are easily outweighed by its strengths.
Its having Great graphic and great story including all its three arkham series

honestly this game is relly old already but it is still amazing the combat feels nice the graphics are top notch the puzzles are really intresting and the batmobile is so much fun and for the price it is an amazing deal
This the best Batman game every made, hell the best superhero game ever made. Such a quality game with such a good story and some amazing side quests. 10/10!!!

I feel pretty weird about Arkham Knight. It's got some really really good parts, and it does a lot of things better than Asylum and City, like the open world, stealth, gadgets, and general combat. At the same time, the execution just isn't that great. Even though the batmobile is fun, it seems like the devs are forcing batmobile sections down your throat 24/7. The main story is good, but it's just too short; while the side missions are either extremely repetitive and last hours, or they're 20 minutes long.

Amazing gameplay and stunning graphics. This game really makes you feel like Batman: the Dark Knight, the Caped Crusader, the World's Greatest Detective.

10/10

Garbage port on release, seems okay now though, UE3 no raytracing yet looks better than most modern games with it. Open world stuff gets  a bit fatiguing and the ending is lacklustre but it grew on me over time. Combat seems on par with Arkham origins and is actually feels and looks worse than the other two games. Driving sections can be annoyong but I ended up enjoying it by the end. (pro tip Battle toggle on in options makes the tank/car controls make more sense.) Riddler is annoying and I couldnt be bothered doing 250+ little puzzles for slightly expanded cutscenes. Nexus mods is your friend here. Boss battles are also Lacklustre to non existent. Despite all my problems with the game it's still worth playing over all especially if you have played the other ones and want to finish the trilogy.

The only complaint I have about this game is that the bosses are straight dog water.
That being said, I love everything else about this game. 
Even the story and Batmobile.

(don't mind time played, I have 65 hrs on xbox with 100% story +dlc completion)

The graphics are also absolutely insane for a 2015 game
2026 - still an amazing game. Beautiful story with twists and game mechanics have aged well
Very good game with great graphics and better game play with good DLCs

Fantastic free roam, gameplay diverse enough to keep you interested throughout the whole story, lets you stick around for the 100% completion or you can start an NG+ after completing the game. Definitely take your time learning the game and getting comfortable with it as the better you get the more enjoyable the game becomes.

Best Batman game for me, finished it several times. It has good graphics, great controller support, good sound, story. For when it came out this was a masterpiece imo. Still the best Batman game, a must have for every Batman and action gamer.
Even a decade later, this is hands down the best comic superhero game, let alone a batman game
The best batman game with the best combat system. Very replay-able plus modding this game is fun too
Amazing game, the whole batman series is awsome and the dlc´s are great.

You know what, People like to follow trends and anything blindly in social media. And Arkham Knight was a victim of that, AK took all of the elements from the pervious games enhanced it and made it 10x more fun and fast action paced. People criticize the story of this game while in my opinion, its actually a good story and follows the same vibe and feel of the previous beloved arkham games. To me this is my personal favorite and I think its the best Batman Arkham game by far. And also get over it bro the batmobile missions aren't that bad, its hella fun. And knowing if you played the previous entries you do the same thing over and over again, the batmobile missions were hella refreshing. Or youre just not into tank fight games whatever that genre is called. The Best Arkham Game of all of them. P.S This games graphics were so ahead of its time that it looks 10x better and puts every triple A title to shame that comes out nowadays.

THIS GAME MAKES YOU FEEL LIKE... But in all honesty this game still slaps hard with its story, gameplay and graphics. Rocksteady's best game ever and one seriously good batman game! 

masterpiece/10!

It may of had a rocky launch way back when, but playing it today it is buttery smooth and by far the slickest iteration of the Arkham franchise. It is, without doubt, the best of the 3 games. It's the ultimate Batman experience. 'Nuff said.
To be old graphics are nice and game is even better with mods.
A good example of why when online reviewers call a game you were interested in "terrible" you should also watch a good chunk of gameplay. This is a good game.

this game was so ahead of its time it still looks insane more than a decade later

also the gameplay is awesome
Well it's a perfect game. I only wish that I could erase my memories of it...So I can play it again!
I just love it! from mastering combo, stealth action and racing.

For its incredible visuals, the game runs perfectly on my aging desktop with its GTX 1660ti, and on my work laptop (specs attached) I could lower the resolution and tolerate a grainy but fairly smooth experience. 

Arkham knight is probably the most beautiful to look at and satisfying to play games in my library. I enjoyed it so much I completed nearly every piece of content within - all the Riddler trophies, side quests, combat scenarios, and most of the stealth ones. I emphatically recommend purchasing the complete Arkham collection, especially when it gets heavily discounted during special events.
Incredibly fun game, keeps up with the times and arkham trilogy reputation.

Batmobile is cool. Maybe a bit much, but cool. Kevin Conroy and Mark Hamill are in this. That's freaking awesome sauce. Gameplay is pretty fun. That's cool. Also only $20. That's pretty cool. Overall pretty cool game I suppose.

[b] Batman: Arkham Knight [/b] is the finale of the acclaimed Arkham trilogy.
This game is visually gorgeous even after a decade and the combat is the most fluent and refined in the franchise. As a result, exploring a gloomy, rain-drenched Gotham City has never felt better.
I also enjoyed the GCPD. Personally putting villains behind bars and being able to watch the cells fill up with criminals is very satisfying.
The Batmobile drives great, sounds powerful and I had a ton of fun using it, though I understand the criticism that it is overused and that the tank battles perhaps feel "un-Batman-like".
Other flaws are a rather underwhelming story and a lack of good Boss Fights.
The latter gets better with the Most Wanted Expansion, but still persists.
Compared to the other two, this one is a bit of a slop to go through because of the Batmobile, but still a good Batman game

Even in 2026, this is still an amazing superhero game, and it’s even easier to get into if you’ve played the previous two games. The missions are easier to find and complete as well, except for the racing sections and the exhausting Riddler challenges. Anyway, this is truly a GOAT-tier game.
Best out of the Arkham trilogy, highly recommended. Great gameplay and story.

The gameplay is phenomenal. I've played Marvel's Spider-Man: Miles Morales, but Batman: Arkham Knight is honestly the superior game. Plus, the visuals still look absolutely stunning today despite its age. A timeless masterpiece! Shame Rocksteady just doesn't create like this anymore.

One of the best story games I've ever played, gushing with content yet doesn't feel overwhelming. 
Has better stealth than most of the new Assassin's Creed games, this game is very ahead of its time.
Small map yet it feels huge when you play it, and you can tell the developers left their unique mark in every nook and cranny of the map. 
I've never played a game quite like this.
The story is amazing and the fighting is even better then the one before! This whole series is so good it's crazy!

gr8 gameplay and graphics

Already 240% this game on another account and excited to do it all again. Absolutely flawless. The ultimate Batman experience. No piece of Batman media will ever come close to surpassing this game and it's already over a decade old. Still amazes me every time I play.

this game is great the one thing that does not make sense is the code thing when your getting one of the infected to get in a door, I had to google. Other than that the game has been great, I highly suggest this.
The game is fun and addicting. Also has a good story to it.

First of all, I want to make one thing clear: even though Batman is overall my favorite hero from any comic book universe, this will not influence the level of admiration I have for this game. And that level of admiration is, indeed, extremely high.

Pros:
✅ I honestly cannot think of another game that made such a massive leap compared to the previous entry in its series. Arkham Knight takes everything introduced in Arkham City and Arkham Asylum, then turns it up by about 500%.
✅ The game is, first and foremost, longer than — at least by my estimate — the two previous entries in the trilogy combined.
✅ You can definitely see why it takes up around 70 GB of disk space. It is spectacular, packed with atmospheric cinematics, memorable sound design, excellent dialogue, and finally, fantastic visual effects that are genuinely jaw-dropping.
✅ I especially mean Gotham itself, which instantly becomes one of the best-looking open worlds I have ever visited in any video game. Simply moving through it is pure pleasure and never gets boring. Even if fast travel existed in this game, I would not use it.
✅ From a gameplay perspective, every single problem I had with the previous entries has been fixed. Moving across rooftops is infinitely smoother, combat is perfectly intuitive, and driving the Batmobile is pure joy.
✅ And yes, the Batmobile. I absolutely did not miss it in the previous games. I did not think it would be anything particularly interesting either. But after finishing Arkham Knight, I cannot help but respect both the idea and the execution of its driving mechanics. I absolutely love causing destruction and tearing through the streets of Gotham at insane speed. And the way the Batmobile is used during missions? Absolutely fantastic.
✅ The best thing about this game, however, is the story. Honestly, I cannot believe how engaging, interesting, and nuanced it is in the way it was written. I struggle to think of a Batman comic that felt more compelling or more epic. The placement of the story within Batman’s life, combined with the careful use of comic book tropes and core events, is phenomenal. Very few licensed games feel this complete.
✅ The ending is absolutely on point, and I will gladly argue with anyone who claims otherwise.
✅ Once again, the rogues’ gallery is basically complete, especially thanks to the additional DLC missions. They are not long, but they are very atmospheric, fairly varied, and genuinely interesting.
✅ The mechanics of playing as members of the Bat-Family, along with the team-up fights, are a feast for the eyes and an incredibly enjoyable addition to the gameplay.
✅ The game is packed with Easter eggs and references to the DC Comics universe. In many places, you can find interactive elements that trigger hidden dialogue paths, as well as iconic locations that unlock journal entries.

Cons:
❌ Once again, in this saga, I did not catch the Riddler and had to watch the full ending — about five minutes — on YouTube. Sorry, but even though the riddles are interesting and demanding, 235 of them is simply too much for me, especially since most require being found across a large, twisted, multi-level map. There are other games to play.
❌ The additional story missions where we play as members of the Bat-Family are short and rather uneven. Some of them were extremely fun for me — Red Hood, Harley Quinn, Catwoman — while others felt much weaker — Batgirl, Nightwing, Robin. I especially disliked the fact that the difficulty mostly comes from them having about 25% of Batman’s health from the main game, which makes it far too easy to die in some stupid fight against regular henchmen.
❌ During one of these DLC missions, the game crashed to desktop once because of an error. Luckily, it happened near the very end of the mission and the save worked properly.
❌ The stealth sequences have been improved compared to the previous entries, but I was still missing one thing: a button for quietly or gently dropping down from a ledge or elevated position. It slightly hurt my strategy whenever I had to just blindly jump down toward an enemy I was trying to sneak up on.

What can I say? Batman: Arkham Knight easily lands among my favorite games of all time.
It is so fantastic that I have no doubt this will be the entry in the saga I return to whenever I want to become my favorite hero, Batman, once again. It basically outclasses the previous games in every single possible way. I especially recommend it to people who are not comic book fans. In my opinion, after playing this game, they might actually become ones and step into the fantastic world of the Dark Knight.

Update (and spoilers): 8/10 The tank fights later in the game have unnecessary, unfun, and infuriating mechanics that don't add to the story.

Poison Ivy doesn't get a tribute or even a "rest in peace" after sacrificing herself (for her plants sure but incidentally also saving Gotham from possibly eternal hell) and after knowing Batman for like what probably 30 years since his vigilante rampage started and helping him multiple times... all she gets "oh well that was a final noble act" from Alfred without a care in his voice. 

He said that like she'd always been a piece of work. Unlike all the other main villains who did downright atrocious serial crimes she's always been in the grey-area... arguably even less bad than what Jason has done (plus her goals were arguably reasonable to an extent and she was mostly just a hindrance to batman sometimes). Her and Batman always had an on-and-off unspoken friendship. In the end, they protected each other. She deserves better!!!
